edges.description: Reduced GBA2 activity impairs glucosylceramide turnover, injuring motor neurons and cerebellum.
edges.target: Glucosylceramide Accumulation and Motor Neuron/Cerebellar Dysfunction

edges.description: Cerebellar neuronal dysfunction manifests as the core ataxia phenotype.
edges.source: Glucosylceramide Accumulation and Motor Neuron/Cerebellar Dysfunction

edges.description: Motor-neuron and corticospinal-tract involvement manifests as spastic paraplegia.
edges.source: Glucosylceramide Accumulation and Motor Neuron/Cerebellar Dysfunction

edges.description: Cerebellar neuronal injury is visible as cerebellar atrophy on imaging.
edges.source: Glucosylceramide Accumulation and Motor Neuron/Cerebellar Dysfunction

edges.description: The same neurodegenerative process includes corpus-callosum atrophy.
edges.source: Glucosylceramide Accumulation and Motor Neuron/Cerebellar Dysfunction

edges.description: Complex GBA2-related neurodegeneration can include cognitive impairment.
edges.source: Glucosylceramide Accumulation and Motor Neuron/Cerebellar Dysfunction

edges.description: Cataract is part of the complex GBA2-associated phenotype, although the intermediate tissue mechanism is unresolved.
edges.source: Glucosylceramide Accumulation and Motor Neuron/Cerebellar Dysfunction

edges.description: Male hypogonadism is part of the complex GBA2-associated phenotype, with unresolved intermediates.
edges.source: Glucosylceramide Accumulation and Motor Neuron/Cerebellar Dysfunction

nodes.description: Cataract is a recurrent extraneurological feature.

evidence.reference_title: Loss of function of glucocerebrosidase GBA2 is responsible for motor neuron defects in hereditary spastic paraplegia.
evidence.snippet:
The overall phenotype was a complex HSP with mental impairment, cataract, and
hypogonadism in males

nodes.description: Cerebellar ataxia, a core feature of the GBA2-related spastic-ataxia phenotype.

different phenotypes like complicated HSP,
recessive cerebellar ataxia or Marinesco-Sj

evidence.snippet:

nodes.description: Mental impairment / intellectual disability is part of the complex phenotype.

nodes.description: Biallelic GBA2 variants reduce the microsomal nonlysosomal glucosylceramidase that converts glucosylceramide to glucose and ceramide, impairing glucosylceramide turnover.

evidence.reference_title: Loss of function of glucocerebrosidase GBA2 is responsible for motor neuron defects in hereditary spastic paraplegia.
evidence.snippet:
GBA2 encodes a microsomal nonlysosomal glucosylceramidase that
catalyzes the conversion of glucosylceramide to free glucose and ceramide

evidence.reference_title: Loss of function of glucocerebrosidase GBA2 is responsible for motor neuron defects in hereditary spastic paraplegia.
evidence.snippet:
we identified
four different mutations in GBA2 (three truncating variants and one missense
variant)

nodes.description: Impaired glucosylceramide turnover injures corticospinal motor neurons and cerebellar neurons, producing the combined spastic paraplegia and cerebellar ataxia with cerebellar and corpus callosum atrophy.
nodes.id: Glucosylceramide Accumulation and Motor Neuron/Cerebellar Dysfunction
